Phase-73.NAME: User Config Page and Amnezia QR Correctness
Phase-73.summary: Approved plan for restoring truthful AmneziaVPN config guidance, preserving working AmneziaWG QR behavior, and cleaning user-visible config page copy and device-limit messaging.
Phase-73.approved-by-operator: Operator confirmed Phase-72 through Phase-76 plan.

scope.in: User /dashboard/config copy, AmneziaWG QR behavior, AmneziaVPN import guidance, selected-device config actions, device-limit exhausted messaging with active tariff display, frontend API typing if needed, and QR/config smoke coverage.
scope.out: Deploy/create-client.sh changes, VPN key generation, AWG profile changes, QR payload mutation without proof, install/deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, MTProto runtime, billing prices, and YooKassa flow.

requirements.requirement-1: AmneziaVPN must not be shown a QR code that the application cannot read.
requirements.requirement-2: AmneziaVPN users must receive concise guidance to download the .conf file and import it into AmneziaVPN.
requirements.requirement-3: AmneziaWG QR must remain available if it is the working QR path and payload parity remains proven.
requirements.requirement-5: Remove "QR, .conf и копирование теперь привязаны к выбранному устройству."
requirements.requirement-6: Replace "Новый конфиг" with "Новый конфиг для вашего устройства".
requirements.requirement-7: Device-limit exhausted messaging must say "Лимит устройств исчерпан согласно вашему Тарифу - [тариф]" using the current active tariff name.

steps.step-1: Audit current Config page QR actions, backend per-device QR endpoints, Phase-70 parity assumptions, and Phase-71 master-detail behavior before edits.
steps.step-2: Identify which QR payload is actually compatible with AmneziaWG and whether AmneziaVPN can import any QR format currently generated.
steps.step-3: Remove or hide non-working AmneziaVPN QR presentation and restore clear .conf import guidance for AmneziaVPN.
steps.step-4: Preserve working AmneziaWG QR and selected-device QR/.conf/Copy actions without payload mutation or key rotation.
steps.step-5: Update Config heading and action copy to "KPN Конфигурация" and "Новый конфиг для вашего устройства".
steps.step-6: Remove the selected-device explanatory sentence requested by the operator while keeping the UX understandable through layout and labels.
steps.step-7: Wire active tariff display into the limit-exhausted message using existing subscription/plan data if present.
steps.step-8: If existing data does not expose a safe display tariff, extend the frontend API type or backend response minimally without changing canonical billing logic.
steps.step-9: Update Phase-73 smoke to reject non-working AmneziaVPN QR, verify AmneziaVPN .conf guidance, verify AmneziaWG QR path, and verify limit message with tariff name.
steps.step-10: Run backend tests only if API shape changes; always run frontend build, Phase-48/57/70/71 smokes, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, diff check, and protected deploy/runtime guard.

data-flow.flow-1: User selects a device on /dashboard/config -> detail panel exposes working AmneziaWG QR, .conf download, and copy actions for that selected device.
data-flow.flow-2: User needs AmneziaVPN -> UI directs them to download .conf and import the file instead of scanning a non-working QR.
data-flow.flow-3: User reaches device limit -> UI reads current plan/subscription display name -> shows the approved tariff-aware exhausted-limit copy.

verification-design.gate-1: Phase-70 QR parity smoke must remain green for working QR payloads.
verification-design.gate-2: Phase-73 smoke must fail if AmneziaVPN QR is visible as a primary scan action while known non-working.
verification-design.gate-3: Phase-48 MIME smoke must remain green for .conf download behavior.
verification-design.gate-4: Backend device/vpn tests must pass if the implementation changes any config or device endpoint response.
verification-design.gate-5: Protected guard must prove deploy/create-client.sh and runtime topology are untouched.

risks.risk-1: Changing QR payload or config text to simplify UX can break working VPN connectivity.
risks.risk-2: Adding tariff copy can accidentally trust frontend price/limit data; only display data is allowed unless backend contract is explicitly extended.
risks.risk-3: Removing explanation text can make config actions less clear; labels must carry enough meaning compactly.

Phase-73.acceptance: Phase-73 is done when the Config page uses approved KPN copy, does not advertise a non-working AmneziaVPN QR, preserves working AmneziaWG QR/.conf/Copy, shows tariff-aware device-limit exhaustion, and passes QR/config/frontend verification.
